topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with the soft, damning finality of a gavel strike. My stomach plummeted. I clicked it open, and the headline screamed my name—a name I hadn't even thought about using in the context of that piece. A cold sweat immediately prickled my hairline. I reread the text, the carefully crafted analysis I’d poured weeks into, and then I looked at the byline: *Attributed to Eleanor Vance*. Eleanor Vance. A complete stranger. My hands began to tremble, the keyboard suddenly feeling slick and alien beneath my fingertips. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small confines of my apartment, the floorboards groaning under my frantic movements. Every shadow seemed to deepen, and the silence of the room amplified the frantic drumming in my chest. I needed to call someone, to demand an explanation, but the words caught in my throat, dissolving into a dry, painful rasp.

[story 2]

I stared at the screen, my breath catching somewhere deep in my lungs. It was like watching a slow-motion car crash in my own life. The article, my voice, my arguments—all someone else’s achievement. A wave of intense heat washed over my face, making my cheeks burn crimson. I pressed the heels of my palms against my eyes, trying to physically block out the words, but the ink seemed to burn itself onto my retinas. I walked to the window, the city lights blurring into streaks of indifferent color. I felt a profound sense of disorientation, as if the ground beneath me had shifted. When I finally turned back to the desk, the papers seemed mocking, pristine and untouchable. I picked up my phone, my fingers stiff, and the sheer effort of dialing a number felt monumental, each button press an act of immense, visible struggle.

[story 3]

The notification popped up, and the air seemed to evacuate the room. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning in protest. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the muscle twitching beneath my skin. It wasn't just disappointment; it was a sharp, sickening lurch in my core. I brought a hand up to grip my forehead, feeling the sudden, unwelcome dampness of perspiration. I started shaking my head slowly, a jerky, involuntary motion, trying to shake the reality loose. *Her* words. *His* insights. The realization settled over me like a heavy, suffocating blanket, pressing down on my chest until breathing became shallow and restricted. I squeezed my eyes shut, wishing with every fiber of my being that I could simply rewind the last twenty-four hours, erasing the moment this betrayal became undeniably real.
